methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.0B last post money.

data.narrative:
ICON operates in the Housing & Construction sector and is currently raising at Series C stage with a post-money valuation of $2.0B, having accumulated $450M+ in total capital raised under founder Kennan Frost's leadership.

Given the limited comp universe available—no public or private comparables were identified for triangulation—valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Applying a typical Series C to next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.7x to the $2.0B last post-money valuation yields an implied value of $3.4B. This represents the most defensible quantitative anchor given data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.9B to $3.9B, with $3.4B as the median point. The range reflects uncertainty inherent in relying on a single methodological approach without market comp validation. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable public or private peers to benchmark unit economics, margin profiles, and growth trajectories within the construction tech space. Future rounds would benefit from establishing a more robust comp set to validate whether stage progression multiples remain appropriate for this sector and company profile.
